prior.content_md:
## LEED for Warehouses and Distribution Centers

LEED certification for warehouses is available under two rating systems:
- **BD+C (Building Design and Construction): New Construction and Major Renovation** — applicable when the workspace is involved in facility fit-out of a new or substantially renovated building. At least 60% of the gross floor area must be complete at certification. *Source: USGBC, March 2026.*
- **O+M (Operations and Maintenance): Existing Buildings** — applicable to existing facilities fully operational and occupied for at least one year. The building must include the entire gross floor area. Recertification occurs every three years via the Arc platform. *Source: USGBC, March 2026.*

LEED v4.1 O+M uses the Arc platform to benchmark performance across five categories: energy, water, waste, transportation, and human experience. For workspaces leasing and occupying logistics facilities, the O+M pathway is the most operationally relevant. *Source: USGBC, March 2026.*

**Volume certification:** For workspaces operating multiple facilities across their trade-lane footprint and certifying several new construction projects, LEED's volume certification program streamlines documentation by focusing on standardized design and construction practices across locations. *Source: USGBC, March 2026.*

**Campus certification:** Where the workspace operates multiple buildings on a single site, LEED Campus Guidance allows shared utilities and campus-wide management policies to be assessed collectively. *Source: USGBC, March 2026.*

## BREEAM for Logistics Facilities

BREEAM is the dominant green building certification framework in the UK and EU logistics market. BREEAM assessments cover energy, water, materials, waste, land use, ecology, pollution, transport, and health and wellbeing. For logistics tenants, the most operationally relevant categories are energy and transport.
*Source: BRE Group (BREEAM), https://www.breeam.com*

## Jurisdiction-Specific Mandatory Requirements (Workspace Trade Lanes)

The following mandatory requirements apply in jurisdictions across the workspace's trade lanes where sourced information is available:

| Jurisdiction | Standard | Status | Applicability |
|---|---|---|---|
| UK (England/Wales) | MEES — EPC E (in force); EPC C by 2027 (proposed); EPC B by 2030 (proposed) | EPC E in force. EPC C uplift: Legal Confirmation Required on current legislative status | Commercial leases. Workspace as tenant: landlord obligation; workspace as sub-lessor: Legal Confirmation Required |
| EU member states | EPBD Recast — zero-emission buildings by 2050, intermediate milestones set by member state transposition | Adopted at EU level; member state transposition ongoing | All buildings including logistics. Specific obligations depend on member state and building type |
| Singapore | BCA Green Mark — mandatory for new commercial buildings above defined thresholds | In force | New construction; workspace facility decisions in Singapore |
| UAE (Abu Dhabi) | Estidama Pearl — mandatory for new buildings | In force | New construction in Abu Dhabi |
| USA (New York City) | Local Law 84/97 — benchmarking (LL84) and carbon emissions caps (LL97) | In force | Buildings >25,000 sqft in NYC |
| USA (Los Angeles) | EWEO — mandatory energy/water benchmarking for commercial buildings >20,000 sqft | In force | Commercial buildings in LA |
| Australia | NABERS — mandatory for offices >1,000 sqm; warehouse expansion under consultation | Offices in force; warehouses: consultation stage | Monitoring only for warehouse operations |

*Sources: UK MEES Guidance, https://www.gov.uk/guidance/domestic-private-rented-property-minimum-energy-efficiency-standard-landlord-guidance; EU EPBD, https://energy.ec.europa.eu; Singapore BCA Green Mark, https://www1.bca.gov.sg/buildsg/sustainability/green-mark-certification-scheme; Estidama Pearl, https://www.abudhabi.ae/en/infrastructure-environment/environment/estidama; NYC LL84/97, https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/benchmarking.page; LA EWEO, https://www.ladbs.org/services/green-building/existing-buildings; NABERS, https://www.nabers.gov.au*

---

*Section 9 (Product-Specific Compliance Status) — Omitted. The workspace does not sell products under its own name within the scope of these building standards.*
